primary_item.title: Half-year Hong Kong stocks report card: shares drop 11% on Fed pivot, lack of AI play
primary_item.summary:
Hong Kong stocks closed out the first half with a loss, ranking the city’s market among the worst performers globally, as a shift by the Federal Reserve and a dearth of pure exposure to artificial intelligence put investors on tenterhooks.
The Hang Seng Index dropped 11 per cent in the January-to-June period, underperforming most of the world’s key equity benchmarks, from the S&P 500 to the Ni

items.title: US Supreme Court boosts Trump’s power to fire officials, but Fed’s Cook keeps job
items.summary:
The US Supreme Court on Monday fortified President Donald Trump’s powers to fire members of independent government agencies, but carved out protections for the Federal Reserve by blocking the firing of Governor Lisa Cook.

primary_item.title: Chinese self-driving firm Momenta eyes US$750m in Hong Kong IPO
primary_item.summary:
Chinese self-driving technology start-up Momenta kicked off bookbuilding on Monday for a HK$5.89 billion (US$751 million) share listing on the Hong Kong stock exchange, testing market sentiment amid a sales slump in China’s car industry.
The company is offering nearly 19.94 million shares at HK$295.60 per share. With 20 shares as the minimum board lot, the minimum investment is HK$5,971.62.
